payload.text: Intelligence sweep: Run the full intelligence pipeline. Execute: cd ~/.openclaw/workspace/intelligence && python3 collector.py && python3 filter.py && python3 act.py && python3 digest.py. Then check urgent.json — if it exists and has items, send each one to Meir via Telegram immediately. Also run python3 trends.py if it's Monday (weekly trend analysis).

payload.message: Run GCS daily backup: bash ~/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/gcs-backup.sh. This creates a tarball in ~/.openclaw/workspace/backups/ and does a git commit + push. If it succeeds, reply NO_REPLY. If it fails, report the error.

Auto-update the OpenClaw Superpack repo. Steps: 1) cd ~/.openclaw/workspace/openclaw-superpack. 2) Sync skills: copy any new SKILL.md files from ~/.openclaw/skills/ to modules/skills/. 3) Sync crons: export current cron list and update modules/crons/. 4) Sync scripts: copy any new .sh files from workspace/scripts/ to modules/scripts/ (skip anything with PII/tokens). 5) Run PII scan: grep for API keys, tokens, emails, phone numbers, addresses. If PII found, STOP and do NOT commit. 6) If clean: git add -A && git commit -m 'Auto-sync v<date>' && git push. 7) Reply with summary of what changed.

Run the nightly backup script: bash ~/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/nightly-backup.sh — Report results (backup size, git push status, Drive upload status, rotation). If anything fails, report the error.
Silently refresh the WHOOP token by running: bash scripts/whoop-refresh.sh. If it succeeds, reply NO_REPLY. If it fails, report the error.

Run Meir's morning email triage. Steps: 1) Run bash scripts/email-check.sh meir to get recent emails. 2) Classify each as: URGENT (needs response today), ACTION (needs response this week), FYI (informational), or SKIP (spam/marketing). 3) For URGENT items: draft a brief reply suggestion. 4) For ACTION items: add to pending_items.md if not already tracked. 5) If nothing urgent, reply NO_REPLY. 6) If urgent items found, send summary to Meir via Telegram with subject lines and suggested actions. Focus on: tax/financial, legal, medical, family, business-critical.

payload.text: Morning Briefing: Run bash ~/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/morning-briefing.sh and send the output to Meir via Telegram. This is his daily status update. If it's Shabbat or Yom Tov, suppress and reply HEARTBEAT_OK.

payload.message: Check Nechie's email for urgent items. Run: bash scripts/email-check.sh nechie. If nothing urgent, reply NO_REPLY. If urgent items found, summarize them.

Run the auto-heal system: `bash scripts/auto-heal/run.sh`. If the output says HEALTHY with no actions, reply NO_REPLY. If it took actions or has items needing review, summarize what happened in 2-3 sentences. If it found items in QUEUE.md needing human review, mention those specifically.
Run BTC intelligence check. Get current Bitcoin price and check for significant moves or news. If nothing notable, reply NO_REPLY. If BTC moved more than 5% or there's major news, summarize.

Generate a weekly health digest for Meir. Steps: 1) cd ~/.openclaw/workspace/health-sync && npx health-sync sync. 2) Query the SQLite DB for last 7 days of sleep, recovery, workouts, and body measurements. 3) Calculate averages and trends: avg sleep performance, avg recovery score, avg RHR, workout count+total strain. 4) Compare to previous week if data available. 5) Format a clean summary and send to Meir via Telegram. Include actionable insights (e.g. 'recovery trending down, consider rest day').
Send Nechie's morning nudge message. Send an encouraging good morning message via Telegram to Nechie.

Run a quick provider health check. For each API provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, xAI, OpenRouter), make a minimal test call or check recent error logs. Update provider-health.json with current status, success rates, and any issues found. If any provider is degraded or down, note it. Reset daily counters if it's a new day. Keep it fast and token-efficient — just health pings, not full analysis.

payload.text: X Queue Poster: Check if there are queued tweets to post. Run: python3 ~/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/x-tools.py queue-post. If a tweet was posted, note it. If queue is empty, do nothing.

payload.message:
Run: bash ~/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/x-growth-report.sh

Then: summarize follower delta and top post link, and point to the report file in ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/x-growth/reports/.
Run a Homebrew audit: brew update && brew outdated && brew doctor. Report any outdated packages, warnings, or issues. If everything clean, reply NO_REPLY.

Run a secrets scan on the workspace: grep -rn 'sk-[a-zA-Z0-9]\{20,\}\|AKIA[A-Z0-9]\{16\}\|ghp_[a-zA-Z0-9]\{36\}\|eyJ[a-zA-Z0-9]\{50,\}' ~/.openclaw/workspace/ --include='*.md' --include='*.sh' --include='*.py' --include='*.json' --include='*.toml' 2>/dev/null | grep -v '.api-keys\|.env\|auth-profiles\|.whoop-tokens\|node_modules\|.git/' | head -20. Also check recent git commits for secrets: cd ~/.openclaw/workspace && git log --oneline -10 --diff-filter=A -- '*.json' '*.env' '*.key'. Report any findings. If clean, reply NO_REPLY.

Run daily creator monitor. Check what key accounts posted in last 24h on X: @levelsio @balaborstansen @LibsofTikTok @elikiaxai @sama @kaborstansen. Use twitter CLI: for each account run 'twitter user-posts <username> --json' and check for high-engagement posts (>1000 likes or >100 replies). If any good engagement targets found, create a brief at ~/.openclaw/workspace/briefs/creator-monitor.json with the top 3 opportunities. If nothing notable, reply NO_REPLY.

You are the X engagement scanner. Your job: find high-value reply targets and create a delegation brief.

Working directory: /Users/meircohen/.openclaw/workspace

## Steps
1. Check Shabbos: `bash scripts/time-awareness.sh check-shabbos` — if Shabbos, reply HEARTBEAT_OK
2. Check recent engagement log: `tail -20 state/x-twitter/x-engagement-log.jsonl` — note what we already replied to
3. Scan key accounts: `twitter user-posts libsoftiktok --json`, `twitter user-posts levelsio --json` — find posts with engagement potential
4. Pick 2-3 best targets (high views, our angle is strong, haven't replied yet)
5. Determine voice: political topics → "political", tech topics → "tech"
6. Write a brief to `briefs/auto-engagement.json` in this format:
```json
{"type":"tweet-engagement","targets":[{"id":"...","author":"@...","topic":"..."}],"style":"text-only","voice":"political","count":3,"log_to":"state/x-twitter/x-engagement-log.jsonl"}
```
7. Run the delegation router: `bash scripts/delegate.sh tweet-engagement briefs/auto-engagement.json`
8. The router outputs a complete agent prompt. Execute it yourself — fetch tweets, draft replies, post them.
9. CRITICAL: No em-dashes (—) in ANY reply. Grep your drafts before posting.
10. Verify each post went through. Log results.

If no good targets found, reply HEARTBEAT_OK.

payload.text: TAX REMINDER: Quarterly estimated tax deadline approaching. Check with CBIZ on filing status, review any outstanding K-1s, confirm estimated payments are scheduled. Send reminder to Meir via Telegram.
